summary: DeVault describes his discovery and rediscovery of linkhut, a social bookmarking tool hosted on SourceHut. After rethinking his relationship with social media and quietly leaving both Mastodon and BlueSky, he found renewed appeal in linkhut as a way to signal-boost interesting articles via RSS feeds. He draws an analogy to SoundCloud's music discovery feature, where you could see what artists you admired were listening to. He encourages others to sign up and share their feeds with him.
key_points: DeVault has quietly left both Mastodon and BlueSky after finding both social experiences wanting
Social bookmarking tools like linkhut paired with RSS offer a compelling alternative to social media for content discovery
The appeal is analogous to SoundCloud circa 2013, where you could discover music through artists' listening habits
He evaluated BlueSky from an OSS perspective and found it both surprisingly open and not open enough
money_quotes: I've been rethinking my relationship social media lately, as some may have inferred from my unannounced disappearance from Mastodon.
I could listen to the music that artists I liked were listening to, and that was amazing for discovering new music.
Similarly, for those of you who enjoy my blog posts, and want to read the stuff I like reading, check out my linkhut feed.

When I set out to evaluate BlueSky for its merits from an OSS framing (findings: both surprisingly open and not open enough), I also took a moment to evaluate the social experience – and found it wanting.
Then I realized that I also felt that way about Mastodon, and that was the end of that.
key_insight: Social bookmarking with RSS feeds offers a quieter, more intentional alternative to social media for sharing and discovering interesting content.
